guideposts.label: Welcome to the live coverage of stage 19 of the Tour de France 2026
guideposts.detail: L’Alpe d’Huez, first leg! After Orcières-Merlette, the peloton head to another iconic venue: the first mountain top finish in the history of the Tour, and the one that has most often featured! It will be reached after the legendary 13.8 km ascent, with its 21 hairpins and its average gradient of 8.1%. The peloton are set to roll from Gap at 14:00, to reach km 0 at 14:15. The winners will be crowne

guideposts.label: Gap, a historic venue of the Tour
guideposts.detail: Passing by France’s highest prefecture is almost compulsory in the Tour, whether during a stage or as a host town. The riders first rolled through Gap in the early morning of 16 July 1905, on their way from Grenoble to Toulon, where Hippolyte Aucouturier took the fourth of his five Tour stage wins. The first time the race stopped in Gap was in 1931. Jeff Demuysere won stage 15, which started in Ni

guideposts.label: Green jersey
guideposts.detail: Battling at the front and making the most of the intermediate sprint, Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) pushed his lead back up to 32 points to Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech): 477 points for the Dane, 445 for the Belgian sprinter. Biniam Girmay (NSN) is still third with 361 points. Today’s stage offers up to 45 points: 25 for the first rider at the intermediate sprint (Le Périer, km 89.4) and 20

guideposts.label: Team classification
guideposts.detail: Lidl-Trek still lead the overall team standings and their margin against UAE Emirates-XRG is up to 22’05’’’’. Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe are in third place overall (+56’10’’).

guideposts.label: Combativity award
guideposts.detail: Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost) races with a distinctive bib today as he was named the most combative rider yesterday. This is his sixth combativity award on the Tour. He took one in 2020, two in 2024, and three this year. Carapaz also won the Super Combativity award in 2024.

guideposts.label: A myth to begin with
guideposts.detail: Alpe d’Huez, which will be at the heart of stages 19 and 20, is a legendary climb that is hard to take your eyes off. But there are many more challenges to be faced in these two decisive days, including some ascents that made Tour history much earlier than the 21-hairpin climb. In 1905, Col Bayard was the first Alpine pass on the route, during stage 4 (Grenoble > Toulon, 348 km). Julien Maitron wa

guideposts.label: Stage 19 of the Tour de France 2026 is ON!
guideposts.detail: The 162-man peloton just passed km 0. Today's route is the shortest in this edition, excluding TTs: 127.9 km.

guideposts.label: Martin-Guyonnet accelerates
guideposts.detail: On his wheel: V. Paret-Peintre, Healy, Carapaz, Gee-West...

guideposts.label: 4 riders set off 4 km from the summit
guideposts.detail: Sean Quinn and Ben Healy are up there (EF Education-EasyPost) with Derek Gee-West (Lidl-Trek) and Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility). UAE Emirates XRG set the pace in the bunch.

guideposts.label: The attackers are caught
guideposts.detail: The pace is brutal to begin the day. Many riders are already distanced: Affini, Kooij, Verstrynge, Godon, Ganna, Veistroffer...

guideposts.label: Around 30 riders at the front
guideposts.detail: Lenny Martinez (Bahrain Victorious) and Adam Yates (UAE Emirates XRG) are there, as well as Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) and Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost). Meanwhile, Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch (UAE Emirates XRG) are dropped.

guideposts.label: Results at Col Bayard (cat 2, km 4.8)
guideposts.detail: 1. V. Paret-Peintre, 5 pts 2. Carapaz, 3 pts 3. A. Johannessen, 2 pts 4. Foss, 1 pt

guideposts.label: Col du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz
guideposts.detail: Tackled for the first time in 1970, Col du Noyer was the protagonist of a legendary feat the following year. At the bottom of the climb, 60 kilometres form the finish at Orcières-Merlette, Luis Ocaña distanced Lucien Van Impe, Joaquim Agostinho and Joop Zoetemelk, while Eddy Merckx was already trailing by a couple of minutes. The Spaniard flew to victory and opened massive gaps: 5’52’’ to Van Impe

guideposts.label: Results at Col du Noyer (cat 1, km 25.2)
guideposts.detail: 1. V. Paret-Peintre, 10 pts 2. Carapaz, 8 pts 3. Arensman, 6 pts 4. Simmons, 4 pts 5. Kuss, 2 pts 6. Riccitello, 1 pt

guideposts.label: Claiming the polka-dot jersey at the eleventh hour?
guideposts.detail: Tadej Pogacar (UAE Emirates XRG) has been leading the mountains classification for 13 stages, since his stage win at Gavarnie-Gèdre past the Col du Tourmalet. Will the Slovenian star claim another polka-dot jersey on the Champs-Élysées, as he did in 2020, 2021, and 2025? Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step) never gave up on the target he had set for himself before the Tour de France and, aft

guideposts.label: 36.3 km in the first hour
guideposts.detail: The attackers rode at a hard pace to open a gap of up to 4 minutes. Now, Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe collaborate with UAE Emirates XRG. The gap is down to 3'30''.

guideposts.label: A battle between two bunches
guideposts.detail: At the front, Visma-Lease a Bike (Armirail), EF Education-EasyPost (Healy, Baudin) and Lidl-Trek (Gee-West) collaborateen route to La Posterle (km 46). In the peloton, UAE Emirates XRG (Politt) and Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe (Van Dijke) control the gap: 3'25''.

guideposts.label: Planckaert and Haller have crashed
guideposts.detail: It was a high speed crash but they both get back up.

guideposts.label: 13 teams yet to win a stage in this Tour
guideposts.detail: Of the 23 teams taking part in this Tour, 10 have secured at least one stage victory and 13 remain winless. Of the latter, three are not part of today’s breakaway: Lotto Intermarché, TotalEnergies and Picnic PostNL. The other ten are indeed at the front: XDS Astana, Bahrain Victorious, Netcompany Ineos, NSN, Movistar Team, Cofidis, Pinarello-Q36.5, Groupama-FDJ United, Tudor and Caja Rural-Seguros

guideposts.label: 11 stage winners at the front
guideposts.detail: Michael Matthews (Revel 2016, Rodez 2017, Romans-sur-Isère 2017, Mende 2022) Mads Pedersen (Saint-Étienne 2022, Limoges 2023, Foix 2026) Richard Carapaz (Superdévoluy 2024, Orcières-Merlette 2026) Thymen Arensman (Luchon-Superbagnères 2025, La Plagne 2025) Ben O’Connor (Tignes 2021, Col de la Loze 2025) Ion Izagirre (Morzine 2016, Belleville-en-Beaujolais 2023) Valentin Paret-Peintre (Mont Ventoux

guideposts.label: Halfway through the stage
guideposts.detail: Rouleurs take strong turn at the front of the breakaway. The gap slightly increases: 3'45''.

guideposts.label: Affini abandons
guideposts.detail: Visma-Lease a Bike's Italian powerhouse was distanced as soon as the stage began.

guideposts.label: Into the final 50 kilometres
guideposts.detail: The attackers set a strong pace and push their lead to 4'15''.

guideposts.label: Now Tarling is distanced
guideposts.detail: Like Healy, he's spent lots of energy to give the breakaway the biggest lead possible: 4'20'' at the moment.

guideposts.label: The average speed after 2 hours: 42.9 km/h
guideposts.detail: The attackers set a strong pace in the second hour. Now Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike) and Derek Gee-West (Lidl-Trek) are distanced.

guideposts.label: The break explodes
guideposts.detail: Many riders are dropped while Bruno Armirail pushes hard on the pedals: Yates, Pedersen, Vacek, O'Connor, Bennett, Howson, Tiberi...

guideposts.label: Derek Gee-West took the lead of the peloton
Carapaz goes first at the summit
guideposts.detail: 1. Carapaz, 5 pts 2. Armirail, 3 pts 3. Foss, 2 pts 4. Arensman, 1 pt

guideposts.label: Plus que 11 dans le peloton Maillot Jaune
The gap at the summit: 3'50''
guideposts.detail: The GC group just caught Alex Baudin. Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM) is bridging the gap on the descent.

guideposts.label: The most iconic mountain top
guideposts.detail: Alpe d’Huez is far from the most used ascent in Tour history: this record belongs to Col du Tourmalet, tackled for the 89th time earlier this year, while today will see the 33rd ascent to Alpe d’Huez. The Pyrenean giant appeared on the Tour as early as 1910, while the Alpine myth waited until 1952 for its debut. But what a debut it was! On 4 July 1952, the peloton set off from Lausanne and covered

guideposts.label: 2022: Pidcock takes Alpe d’Huez glory
guideposts.detail: The last finish at Alpe d’Huez before this year’s double visit was four years ago, on stage 12 of the 2022 edition. It was 14 July and Tom Pidcock delivered the fireworks. The stage started from Briançon and headed immediately to Col du Galibier, a magnificent summit to battle for the breakaway. Climbers set off on the ascent… and Tom Pidcock attacked towards the summit. The British star joined th

guideposts.label: Why is Alpe d’Huez known as ‘the Dutch mountain’?
guideposts.detail: Of the 13 summit finishes on this legendary mountain that took place between 1976 and 1989, eight ended in victory for a Dutch rider: the winners were Joop Zoetemelk (2), Hennie Kuiper (2), Peter Winnen (2), Steven Rooks and Gert-Jan Theunisse. 5.5 kilometres from the summit lies the legendary “Dutch bend”; the seventh of the 21 hairpin bends the riders will tackle. Alpe d’Huez has also seen Dutch

guideposts.label: Arensman accelerates
guideposts.detail: Martinez is the quickest to react. Kuss and Carapaz chase them down. 12.5 km to go.

guideposts.label: Ayuso and Skjelmose are dropped
guideposts.detail: Grossschartner sets the pace.

guideposts.label: Pogacar attacks 13 kilometres from the finish
guideposts.detail: The Slovenian is 3 minutes behind the leaders. Prodhomme sets the pace behind him. On his wheel: Seixas, Del Toro, Evenepoel, Skjelmose, Van Gils.

guideposts.label: Kuss ups the ante
guideposts.detail: Carapaz and Martinez follow the US climber 11.5 km from the summit.

guideposts.label: Pogacar catches up to Yates
guideposts.detail: They are 2'45'' behind the lead trio.

guideposts.label: Carapaz attacks 10.5 km to the summit
guideposts.detail: Kuss and Martinez resist.

guideposts.label: Carapaz attacks again 9 km from the summit
guideposts.detail: Martinez keeps up, Kuss is distanced.

guideposts.label: Kuss gets back to Carapaz and Martinez
guideposts.detail: Pogacar trails by 1'50''. +2'40'' Seixas - Del Toro group

guideposts.label: Carapaz attacks again
guideposts.detail: Martinez follows again. Kuss returns again. The gap to Pogacar is down to 1'15''. 7 kilometres to go.

guideposts.label: The three leaders don't work well together
guideposts.detail: Sepp Kuss tries to up the ante 6 kilometres from the summit. Tadej Pogacar trails by 1'05''. The Evenepoel group are 1'30'' behind him.

guideposts.label: And another attack by Carapaz
guideposts.detail: Martinez follows him. And Kuss gets back.

guideposts.label: 3 kilometres to the summit
guideposts.detail: Kuss attacks, Carapaz counters... He opens a gap.

guideposts.label: Pogacar passes Kuss and catches Martinez
guideposts.detail: Carapaz is 5 seconds ahead.

guideposts.label: Evenepoel has set off from the chase group
guideposts.detail: He trails by 2'15''. +2'30'' Seixas, Riccitello, Del Toro, Skjelmose, Tejada

guideposts.label: Del Toro attacks
guideposts.detail: Seixas stays with Riccitello.

guideposts.label: Pogacar and Martinez catch Carapaz
guideposts.detail: 1.8 km to go.

guideposts.label: Pogacar accelerates...
guideposts.detail: Carapaz and Martinez resist.

guideposts.label: Tadej Pogacar wins stage 19 of the Tour de France 2026
guideposts.detail: His 5th in this Tour, his 26th since 2020.

guideposts.label: Top 5
guideposts.detail: 1. Tadej Pogacar (UAE Emirates XRG) 2. Lenny Martinez (Bahrain Victorious), +6'' 3. Richard Carapaz (EF Education-EasyPost), +9'' 4. Sepp Kuss (Visma-Lease a Bike), +1'14'' 5. Tobias Johannessen (Uno-X Mobility), +2'07''

special_markers.label: Col du Noyer: from Ocaña to Carapaz
Pogacar attacks 13 kilometres from the finish
Carapaz attacks 10.5 km to the summit
Carapaz attacks again 9 km from the summit
Kuss gets back to Carapaz and Martinez
Pogacar passes Kuss and catches Martinez
Evenepoel has set off from the chase group
